summary: Closed, unmerged PR that decompiled Mewtwo's SpecialHi/Teleport into a new matching C file, replacing most of the corresponding ftmewtwo assembly with named C callbacks and action handlers. The work also added Mewtwo SpecialHi state vars, expanded ftmewtwo attributes/action-state naming, updated the Mewtwo action-state table to point at named functions, and touched broader fighter state-var naming. Review feedback objected to uppercase struct member names, reinforcing the project convention that uppercase identifiers are for enum-like constants rather than struct fields.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftMewtwo/ftMewtwo_SpecialHi.c with 632 lines implementing Mewtwo Teleport/SpecialHi start, travel/zoom, lost/end, ground-to-air, air-to-ground, animation, IASA, physics, collision, and GFX callback routines.
Removed roughly 1223 lines of corresponding Mewtwo SpecialHi assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s while leaving the file present for remaining Mewtwo asm.

Updated the Mewtwo action-state table entries from raw labels such as lbl_8014536C/lbl_801453D0 to named callbacks such as ftMewtwo_SpecialHiStart_Anim, ftMewtwo_SpecialHiStart_Phys, ftMewtwo_SpecialHi_Coll, ftMewtwo_SpecialAirHiLost_Coll, and related variants.
Changed obj_files.mk to link src/melee/ft/chara/ftMewtwo/ftMewtwo_SpecialHi.c.o before the remaining asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s.o.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftMewtwo/ftMewtwo_StateVars.h with ftMewtwoSpecialHi fields including travelFrames, stickX, stickY, unk4, velX, velY, and groundVelX, then exposed ftMewtwoStateVars through Fighter as mewtwoVars[0] at 0x2340.

Expanded ftmewtwo.h with ftMewtwoAction enum values for Mewtwo action states, a FTMEWTWO_SPECIALHI_COLL_FLAG macro, and named Mewtwo special attribute fields for Shadow Ball, Teleport, and Disable-related values.
Renamed ftMewTwoAttributes to ftMewtwoAttributes in touched Mewtwo code and renamed x2234 to x2234_shadowBallCharge in Fighter's Mewtwo special attributes.
Changed Mewtwo OnLoad item kind registrations from numeric constants 0x6E/0x70 to It_Kind_Mewtwo_Disable and It_Kind_Mewtwo_ShadowBall.
Added CLIFFCATCH_O(fp) and CLIFFCATCH_U(fp) macros in fighter.h and used CLIFFCATCH_O(fp) in Mewtwo SpecialAirHi collision code.

Touched several other character files and state-var headers, especially Ness/Luigi/Mario/GameWatch/Captain, mostly for state-var member naming/casing consistency such as SpecialHi to specialHi and CliffCommon to cliffCommon.

include/functions.h was rewritten with equal added/deleted line counts and gained commented declarations/mentions for Mewtwo SpecialHi helper names; the exact motivation for the wholesale rewrite is not evident from the slice.
smart_moves: Kept linker order explicit by adding ftMewtwo_SpecialHi.c.o immediately adjacent to ftmewtwo.c.o and before the remaining ftmewtwo.s.o, a common safe pattern when partially replacing an asm file with C.
Converted action-state table pointers to descriptive callback names, making future Mewtwo work searchable by move phase and callback type instead of raw labels.
Separated per-action runtime state into ftMewtwo_StateVars.h and Fighter.mewtwoVars[0], matching the existing character-specific state-var pattern used by other fighters.
Centralized the repeated action-state preservation bits into FTMEWTWO_SPECIALHI_COLL_FLAG, reducing repetition in ground-air transition calls while preserving exact flags.
Used named item-kind constants for Mewtwo's Disable and Shadow Ball item registrations instead of raw numeric IDs.
Kept exact-looking float constants such as HALF_PI32 and DEG_TO_RAD decimal values in the decompiled C, which is often important for matching PowerPC float codegen.
Recorded decomp.me scratch links beside many functions, which gives future workers provenance for individual matches.
decomp_lessons: When replacing only part of a character asm file, remove just the matched text-range functions from asm, add the C object in linker order, and update any remaining asm data tables to point to the new C symbols.

Action-state table callbacks should be renamed by fighter, action state, and callback phase: examples include ftMewtwo_SpecialHiStart_Anim, ftMewtwo_SpecialHiStart_IASA, ftMewtwo_SpecialHiStart_Phys, and ftMewtwo_SpecialHiStart_Coll.
Fighter state variables at 0x2340 should live behind character-specific state-var unions, e.g. Fighter.mewtwoVars[0].specialHi.travelFrames, not anonymous raw x2340 fields.

Matching C may require preserving non-idiomatic patterns seen in this diff, including repeated assignments like fp = fp = getFighter(fighter_gobj), unused local variables, inline helper functions, and exact float literals.

Ground/air transition handlers need to preserve the current animation frame and use the correct collision/action-state preservation flags, as seen in Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC calls with fp->x894_currentAnimFrame and FTMEWTWO_SPECIALHI_COLL_FLAG.
Collision callbacks for teleport-like movement need separate handling for ground checks, ledge checks, wall/ceiling/floor envFlags, and vector-angle clamps against self velocity.
Reviewers care about project-wide naming style even in matching PRs; struct member names that are descriptive still need to follow lowercase/member casing conventions.
naming_conventions: Use ftMewtwo, not ftMewTwo, for Mewtwo type names in touched code: ftMewtwoAttributes replaced ftMewTwoAttributes.
Action-state enum constants use uppercase AS_MEWTWO_* names, e.g. AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALHI_START and AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALAIRHI_LOST.
Struct and union members should not use all-uppercase descriptive chunks; reviewer PsiLupan stated: "Uppercase names are typically reserved for things like enums. Don't use it for struct members."
State-var union members use lower camel-case move names such as specialHi and specialN, not SpecialHi or SpecialN.
Common state-var members were moved toward lowercase names, e.g. CliffCommon to cliffCommon.

Named special-attribute fields in this PR used offset-prefixed names such as x50_MEWTWO_TELEPORT_DURATION and x0_MEWTWO_SHADOWBALL_CHARGE_CYCLES, but the uppercase portions were specifically challenged in review for struct members.
Callback naming pattern: ftMewtwo_SpecialHi{Phase}_{Anim|IASA|Phys|Coll}, with Air and Lost variants encoded in the function name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the matched Mewtwo SpecialHi assembly block from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s while retaining later asm and data in the same file.
Updated asm action-state table .4byte entries from local labels to C function symbols, avoiding duplicate code while keeping asm data layout intact.
Added the new C object to obj_files.mk before the remaining asm object so symbol addresses/link order could continue to match.
Preserved exact action-state IDs through enum values starting at AS_FTCOMMON = 340 and subsequent AS_MEWTWO_* entries.
Used exact-looking constants and macros for float-sensitive code: HALF_PI32 = 1.5707963705062866f and DEG_TO_RAD = 0.01745329238474369f.

Used decomp-friendly non-idiomatic local declarations and assignments, including redundant getFighter/getFtSpecialAttrsD/getFtColl assignments and unused locals, presumably to influence register allocation/codegen; the slice does not include explicit reviewer confirmation of this tactic.
Factored repeated state setup into inline helpers ftMewtwo_SpecialHi_SetVars and ftMewtwo_SpecialHiLost_SetVars, likely to match inlined original code while avoiding repeated source text.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Uppercase names are typically reserved for things like enums. Don't use it for struct members.
review_feedback.applies_to: New ftMewtwoAttributes member names such as x0_MEWTWO_SHADOWBALL_CHARGE_CYCLES and related uppercase attribute fields.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that a separate branch was up to date with this work and had a clean history, and offered to PR it if requested.
review_feedback.applies_to: PR cleanup/continuation after this closed unmerged PR.

follow_up_queries: Find whether ribbanya's clean-history branch for PR #450 was later opened or merged under another PR.
Search later Mewtwo PRs for ftMewtwoAttributes member renames away from uppercase attribute field names after PsiLupan's review comment.
Check current ftMewtwo_SpecialHi.c for whether the redundant assignments and exact float constants survived, indicating they were required for matching.
Search for subsequent cleanup of include/functions.h from this PR, since the diff slice shows a wholesale 8904-line rewrite with unclear intent.
